revision.comment: Grundinfos
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Kopf) des Schafs'' }} '''Seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der neun [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Deneb Algedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] wurde Seret in der Sethos-Schrift als 20. Dekan geführt; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] erschien Seret an 19. Stelle. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Kopf) des Schafs'' }} '''Seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der neun [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Deneb Algedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] erschien Seret in der Sethos-Schrift an 20. Stelle; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] wurde Seret als 19. Dekan geführt, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet III]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Kopf) des Schafs'' }} '''Seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der neun [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Deneb Algedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repäsentierte Seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 19. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet III]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Seret (Dekan)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セレット]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Seret (Dekan)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セレット]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[en:draft:Seret (Dekan)]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 pages]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:qq]]

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セレット]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[en:draft:Seret (Dekan)]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Seret (Decan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=draft|12=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi|12=draft|12=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:セレット]] [[en:draft:Seret (Dekan)]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] [[sn:Seret (Decan)]]

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=draft|12=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi|12=draft|12=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Seret (Decan)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:セレット]] [[en:draft:Seret (Dekan)]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] [[sn:Seret (Decan)]]

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Kopf) des Schafs'' }} '''Seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der neun [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Deneb Algedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 19. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet III]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|qq=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Deneb Algedi|de|Deneb Algedi|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. ''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is [[:en:Delta Capricorni|Deneb Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is [[:en:Delta Capricorni|Deneb Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2272336]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITEL = Seret |NAME = S29-D21:t-F8 N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Seret
''Sr.t''
''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is [[:en:Delta Capricorni|Deneb Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Seret (Dekan)|version=234410605|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セレット]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2272336}}

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Seret (Dekan)|version=234410605|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セレット]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2272336}}

''(Head) of the Sheep'' }} '''Seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised nine {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is [[:en:Delta Capricorni|Deneb Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Seret (Dekan)|version=234410605|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セレット]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2272336}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is [[:en:Delta Capricorni|Deneb Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Seret (Dekan)|version=234410605|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:セレット]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] {{wikidata link|Q2272336}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

The most prominent {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} in this decan is {{ill|Delta Capricorni|en|Delta Capricorni|WD=Q1054991|lt=Deneb Algedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus|en|Capricornus|WD=Q10535|lt=Capricornus (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Seret represented the 19th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet III|de|6. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Seret (Dekan)|version=234410605|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:セレット]] [[de:Seret (Dekan)]] {{wikidata link|Q2272336}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
